title: Phenomenon 70
wikitext:
{{Phenomenon Dreyfus Scale|threat=2|frequency=1|frequencydesc='''Casper-Bray Rifts''' rarely occur within the levels, which are most often recorded far from an average ingress point.|hazard=1|hazarddesc='''Casper-Bray Rifts''' may take one to undocumented or dangerous levels if used unknowingly.|enigma=4|enigmadesc='''Casper-Bray Rifts''' yet stand as one of the least understood phenomena in the Backrooms.}}{{VoidedThemeV2}}{{Background|file=9223372036854775807 Void.png}}__NOTOC__ ==Description== {{Simple Image Block|image=Casper-Bray Rift.png|caption=A '''Casper-Bray Rift''' found in [[Level 45]].}} '''Phenomenon 70''', also known as “'''Casper-Bray Rifts'''”, is a spatial anomaly appearing against the fabric of space-time. Those rifts often take the form of a white, glowing, floating point. Rifts are thought to be made of something other than regular matter and are thought to be a cluster of an unidentified hub category between fermions and bosons. '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' interact with bosons in a special way; while the rift emits and reflects electromagnetic radiation, this process is thought to happen on a surrounding field called "Rift Field," filling an area depending on the rift’s size. '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' attract mass in a polar approach, with it theorized to have an opposite sign for Higgs bosons; thus, rifts are said to have a “negative mass.” Unlike gravitational forces, this negative mass allows rifts to remain in place motionless, while warping the space around them via a logarithmic directional propensity. {{Simple Image Block|image=Level_305.png|caption=A '''Casper-Bray Rift''' causing [[Level 305]] to deform, leading to [[Level 410]].}} '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' disruption of space can vary with the intensity of the rifts, which is measured by how strongly their “Rift Field” counteracts electromagnetic rays; this may correlate with the rifts’ size on regular occasions. Intensity is directly increased by the more “negative mass” it has; due to this, a rift will get smaller and weaker on physical interactions. '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' serve as interlevel transportation; interaction with a rift causes one to change location depending on the principal or abstract patterns. Usually, a rift will lead one into the level range of a few before or after on the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]]; this happens in the majority of the encounters on numbered levels. Abstract patterns such as undetermined categories or anomalous levels, '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' might deform environmental features associated with similarities between tied levels. Even though theorized, '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' might allow us to discover new kinds of subclusters and hidden relations between levels, but for now, most features of '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' remain an enigma. ==Recorded Examples== In regular examples such as numbered levels, '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' have been found to have a 54% predisposition to descend and a 46% predisposition to ascend. Additionally, rifts are more likely to be found at higher numbered levels than lower numbered levels, with it being nearly unimaginable to find within The Main Nine Levels; also, inescapable levels are thought to lack any kind of rifts as well. Anomalous levels and levels with undetermined relations have had unusual records listed. {| class="fandom-table" |+Recorded examples of '''Phenomenon 70'''. !'''Ranged Relations''' !'''Singular Relations''' !'''Undetermined Relations''' |- |[[Level 630]] ⇌ [[Level 799]] |[[Level 305]] ↔ [[Level 410]] |[[Level 307]] → [[Level 306]] |- |[[Level 799]] ⇌ [[Level 208]] |[[Level 483]] ↔ [[Level 484]] |[[Level 399]] → [[Level 398]] |- |[[Level 208]] ⇌ [[Level 306]] |[[The Beginning]] ↔ [[The End]] |[[Level 422]] → [[Level 151]] |} ===Ranged Relations=== Ranged relations are a cluster of multiple levels, with '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' behaving on a specific fundamental that’s undetermined for us yet. These levels are somewhat linked to each other due to unknown reasons, and rifts will transition one into a level that’s within the range on all occasions, though this seems not to affect other egresses in many cases, so those ranges stand available and unrestricted. ===Singular Relations=== Singular relations are interlinked levels causing the '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' to only traverse amongst the two; one’ll traverse to the other level and come back to the starting level in all even usages; most famous examples are between [[Level 305]] and [[Level 410]], [[Level 483]] and [[Level 484]], and [[The Beginning]] with [[The End]]. The definite process remains lacking, but the majority suggests that these levels might have restricted rifts due to the tulpaefiction of the connection between levels becoming too dominant. ===Undetermined Relations=== Undetermined relations remain the least understood amongst the three, which causes the levels’ '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' to only lead into a point that’s irreversible on the other side. One may use the rift to reach the other linked level only with a one-way route, without the ability to use a rift to come back to the previous point. ===Documentation=== '''Casper-Bray Rifts''' documentation is still incomplete, and there are thought to be many more examples of relations waiting to be found; it’s highly advised to add newly found interlinks into the page for further research.
[[Uncharted Territory|What’s below level 0?]]
